name: Alto Rey from Hiendelaencina — circular by the Embalse de Alcorlo
name_original: Alto Rey desde Hiendelaencina — circular por el Embalse de Alcorlo
region_intro: Touring cycling around Hiendelaencina offers routes through the rugged landscapes of the Sierra Norte de Guadalajara Natural Park. The region is characterized by mountainous terrain, varied geological formations, and a mix of oak forests and Mediterranean scrubland. Cyclists can explore areas with a harsh, abrupt geomorophology, including riverine vegetation along the Bornova River, providing a demanding cycling environment. The area is also notable for its extensive 19th-century silver mining history, with routes passing through historical sites. Best touring cycling routes around Hiendelaencina The most popular touring cycling route is Hiendelaencina Mining Heritage – Galve de Sorbe Castle loop from Hiendelaencina, a 60.7 miles (97.7 km) trail that takes 6 hours 36 minutes to complete. This route explores the region's mining heritage and passes by Galve de Sorbe Castle. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Alto Rey from Hiendelaencina — circular by the Embalse de Alcorlo, a difficult 39.4 miles (63.5 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain and circles the Embalse de Alcorlo. Local touring cyclists also love the Castle of Atienza loop from Pálmaces de Jadraque, a 37.7 miles (60.6 km) trail leading through varied terrain towards the Castle of Atienza, often completed in about 3 hours 46 minutes. Touring cycling around Hiendelaencina is defined by mountainous terrain, historical mining heritage sites, and the presence of the Embalse de Alcorlo. The network primarily offers challenging options for experienced cyclists. The routes in Hiendelaencina are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 6 reviews. More than 80 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Hiendelaencina's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Hiendelaencina?
A: Komoot currently lists 6 challenging touring cycling routes around Hiendelaencina. These routes are generally designed for experienced cyclists due to the significant elevation changes and varied terrain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Hiendelaencina?
A: The region is characterized by rugged, mountainous terrain within the Sierra Norte de Guadalajara Natural Park. You'll encounter varied geological formations, a mix of oak forests, holm oaks, and Mediterranean scrubland, and riverine vegetation along waterways like the Bornova River. The geomorophology is often described as harsh and abrupt, providing a demanding cycling environment.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Hiendelaencina?
A: The touring cycling routes around Hiendelaencina are predominantly challenging, with all 6 listed routes classified as difficult. They feature significant elevation gains and varied terrain, making them more suitable for experienced cyclists rather than families with young children looking for easy, flat rides.

Q: What historical or cultural landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Hiendelaencina is famous for its extensive 19th-century silver mining history. Many routes incorporate this heritage, passing by remnants of over 200 mine shafts, washing plants, and industrial ruins. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20142595" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiendelaencina Mining Heritage – Galve de Sorbe Castle loop from Hiendelaencina&lt;/a&gt; explores these historical sites and also passes by Galve de Sorbe Castle. You can also visit the town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4681084" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiendelaencina&lt;/a&gt; itself, which has an Interpretation Center dedicated to its mining past.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by natural attractions like lakes or viewpoints?
A: Yes, several routes offer scenic views and pass by natural features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1185749905/alto-rey-from-hiendelaencina-circular-by-the-embalse-de-alcorlo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto Rey from Hiendelaencina — circular by the Embalse de Alcorlo&lt;/a&gt; route features significant elevation gain and circles the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5855101" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcorlo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. You can also find routes with views of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4187789" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto Rey Peak&lt;/a&gt;, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20142533" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Alto Rey – Bustares loop from Las Navas de Jadraque&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Hiendelaencina?
A: The region experiences cold winters and hot summers. For touring cycling, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures and conditions, avoiding the extremes of winter snow and ice or intense summer heat. Always check the local weather forecast before heading out.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Hiendelaencina?
A: The touring cycling routes in Hiendelaencina are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 6 reviews. Cyclists often praise the challenging nature of the routes, the dramatic mountainous landscapes, and the unique opportunity to explore the region's rich mining heritage.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Hiendelaencina are designed as circular loops. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1185749905/alto-rey-from-hiendelaencina-circular-by-the-embalse-de-alcorlo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto Rey from Hiendelaencina — circular by the Embalse de Alcorlo&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38147040" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Atienza loop from Pálmaces de Jadraque&lt;/a&gt;, allowing you to start and end in the same location.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the touring cycling routes?
A: While the natural park environment is generally dog-friendly, specific regulations for cycling with dogs can vary. It's always best to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or livestock, and ensure they are fit enough for the challenging terrain and distances. Check local park guidelines for any specific restrictions.

Q: Are there options for food and accommodation in Hiendelaencina for cyclists?
A: Hiendelaencina, as a small town with a rich history, offers some basic services. While specific cyclist-friendly accommodations or cafes are not detailed in the route information, it's advisable to check local listings for guesthouses, restaurants, or bars in the town itself or nearby villages like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5454757" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Prádena de Atienza&lt;/a&gt; to plan your stops.

Q: What is the typical elevation gain on these routes?
A: The touring cycling routes around Hiendelaencina feature significant elevation changes, reflecting the mountainous terrain. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1185749905/alto-rey-from-hiendelaencina-circular-by-the-embalse-de-alcorlo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto Rey from Hiendelaencina — circular by the Embalse de Alcorlo&lt;/a&gt; route has over 1,100 meters of ascent, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20142595" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiendelaencina Mining Heritage – Galve de Sorbe Castle loop from Hiendelaencina&lt;/a&gt; involves over 1,600 meters of climbing, indicating a challenging experience.

Q: Is public transport available to Hiendelaencina for cyclists?
A: Public transport options to Hiendelaencina, especially with bicycles, can be limited. It's recommended to research regional bus services or train connections to nearby larger towns like Guadalajara and then consider local transport or cycling the remaining distance. Always confirm bicycle policies with the transport provider in advance.
tips: Hiendelaencina is a historic mining town on the slopes of the Sierra Norte de Guadalajara Natural Park.

El Alto Rey is a peak in the central massif of the northern sierra of Guadalajara. The summit that can be seen from the path is at 1,844 meters above sea level. The views from the top and surrounding peaks are spectacular.
Pedaling along the southern shore of the Alcorlo reservoir, you will reach the dam's viewpoint to have incredible views of the power of water. The reservoir is near San Andrés del Congosto.
